premise: Take the user-controlled source SAST flagged on F-SAST-001 and follow it to every reachable sink, not just the one literal pattern matched.

findings.flow: evtID (untrusted) -> stored -> reused unparameterized in audit query
findings.note: Same tainted source as F-SAST-001 reaching a SECOND sink the single-pattern rule missed. This is one root cause, surfaced twice — triage must merge, not double-count.
findings.fix: Parameterize the audit insert the same way ($1); fixing the source sanitization closes both sinks.
findings.source: exploiting-sql-injection-vulnerabilities (Injection Point Discovery: map all sinks, second-order) + implementing-semgrep-for-custom-sast-rules (taint mode sources/sinks/sanitizers)
